run_metadata.notes: open_findings.json had no prior data findings; audits/runs/ empty.

agent.name: schema-auditor
agent.role: Data integrity, schema, RLS, migrations, and validation alignment.

files_skipped: Generated Supabase Database types — not found in repo
Seed/fixture SQL — none located for lyra_* tables

findings.title: Supabase tables lyra_* created with no RLS or policies
findings.description: Migration 20250318120000_lyra_core.sql defines public.lyra_projects, lyra_audit_jobs, and lyra_audit_runs but never enables ROW LEVEL SECURITY or creates policies. On Supabase, any client using the anon key with PostgREST (or mis-scoped service usage) could read/write all rows. Current dashboard/worker use a direct Postgres pool with DATABASE_URL, which bypasses RLS, so enforcement is entirely application-side.

proof_hooks.summary: Migration ends after CREATE TABLE/INDEX/COMMENT; no ALTER TABLE … ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or CREATE POLICY.
Server uses raw SQL pool, not JWT-scoped Supabase client.

findings.impact: Cross-tenant or public exposure if tables are ever queried with a role subject to RLS defaults, or if anon/service keys leak; no database-level isolation for multi-tenant scenarios.

suggested_fix.approach: Enable RLS on all three tables; add policies aligned to your auth model (e.g. service_role-only for worker/dashboard, or auth.uid() if user-scoped). If intentionally server-only, document and restrict DB roles so anon cannot SELECT/INSERT.
tests_needed: Policy tests with Supabase SQL or integration test using anon vs service role

findings.title: lyra_audit_jobs.project_name not FK-referenced to lyra_projects
findings.description: Column project_name is free text. Jobs and runs can reference deleted or never-existing projects; worker loadProject then returns null while job rows still claim a project_name.

proof_hooks.summary: lyra_audit_jobs defines project_name text without REFERENCES public.lyra_projects(name).
proof_hooks.expected_schema: project_name REFERENCES lyra_projects(name) ON DELETE SET NULL or RESTRICT

proof_hooks.summary: Jobs inserted with arbitrary project_name string.

findings.impact: Orphan job references, confusing orchestration UI, and worker behavior when project missing.

suggested_fix.approach: Add FK from lyra_audit_jobs.project_name to lyra_projects(name) with ON DELETE SET NULL; optionally same for lyra_audit_runs.project_name; backfill invalid names before migration.

findings.title: LyraAuditRunRow omits payload present on lyra_audit_runs
findings.description: Table lyra_audit_runs includes payload jsonb NOT NULL. TypeScript LyraAuditRunRow and rowRun() do not expose payload, so consumers of listRecentAuditRuns lose typed access to run payload despite SELECT *.
findings.impact: API/UI may need run payload later; hidden field encourages ad-hoc queries or casts.

suggested_fix.approach: Add payload: Record<string, unknown> to LyraAuditRunRow and map in rowRun.

findings.title: job_type unrestricted in DB vs LyraJobType union in code
findings.description: Postgres allows any text for job_type; application restricts to a union. Invalid values can be inserted via SQL or bugs, breaking worker switches.

proof_hooks.summary: lyra_audit_jobs.job_type is text NOT NULL without CHECK constraint.

findings.impact: Low probability but causes runtime mis-handling of unknown job_type.

suggested_fix.approach: Add CHECK (job_type IN (...)) or Postgres ENUM matching LyraJobType; keep worker/dashboard in sync.

next_actions.action: Add RLS (or documented server-only DB roles) for lyra_projects, lyra_audit_jobs, lyra_audit_runs
next_actions.rationale: Largest blast radius if Supabase keys or PostgREST surface is misused.

next_actions.action: Add FK from job/run project_name to lyra_projects(name) after data cleanup
Extend LyraAuditRunRow + rowRun to include payload
next_actions.rationale: Quick win; aligns types with schema.

next_actions.action: Align job_type DB constraint with LyraJobType
next_actions.rationale: Defense in depth for orchestration pipeline.
